name: Sierra de Alhama and Old Railway Loop from Periana
name_original: Sierra de Alhama – circular desde Periana por antiguo tren cremallera
description: This moderate 16.5-mile mountain bike route from Periana explores the Sierra de Alhama, following an old rack railway with reservoir views.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Periana offers a diverse landscape characterized by the Sierra de Tejeda and Sierra de Alhama mountain ranges, alongside agricultural plains and views of reservoirs like La Viñuela. The terrain includes varied elevations, from challenging mountain ascents to more gentle paths through valleys and along old railway lines. This region provides a mix of natural surfaces and established tracks suitable for mountain biking. Best mountain bike trails around Periana The most popular mountain bike trail is Sierra de Tejeda – Circular from Puente, a 22.2 miles (35.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 48 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the mountainous terrain of the Sierra de Tejeda. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Sierra de Alhama and Old Railway Loop from Periana, a moderate 16.5 miles (26.6 km) path. This route combines mountain views with sections along a former railway line. Local mountain bikers also love the View of Polje de Zafarraya – Zafarraya Plain loop from Zafarraya, a 27.0 miles (43.5 km) trail leading through the Zafarraya Plain, often completed in about 4 hours 24 minutes. Mountain biking around Periana is defined by mountain ranges, agricultural plains, and reservoir views. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy paths to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Periana are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 600 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Periana's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Periana?
A: There are over 40 mountain bike trails around Periana, offering a diverse range of experiences from gentle paths to challenging mountain ascents. The komoot community has explored these routes over 600 times.

Q: What is the overall difficulty level of mountain biking in Periana?
A: The mountain biking trails around Periana offer a varied experience. While there is 1 easy route, the majority are moderate (25 routes) or difficult (14 routes), catering to riders looking for more challenging terrain and significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Periana?
A: Yes, there is 1 easy mountain bike route available around Periana. These trails typically feature less challenging terrain and elevation, making them suitable for beginners or those seeking a more relaxed ride.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders in Periana?
A: Absolutely. Periana offers 14 difficult mountain bike trails, often exploring the mountainous terrain of ranges like the Sierra de Tejeda. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994115133/sierra-de-tejeda-circular-from-puente" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Tejeda – Circular from Puente&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 22.2 miles (35.8 km) with significant elevation gain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Periana?
A: The terrain around Periana is diverse, characterized by the Sierra de Tejeda and Sierra de Alhama mountain ranges, agricultural plains, and views of reservoirs like La Viñuela. You'll encounter a mix of natural surfaces and established tracks, with varied elevations from challenging ascents to more gentle paths, including sections along old railway lines.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Periana?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes around Periana are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994114264/sierra-de-alhama-and-old-railway-loop-from-periana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Alhama and Old Railway Loop from Periana&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate option that combines mountain views with former railway sections.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Periana?
A: The mountain bike trails in Periana are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 60 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse landscapes, from mountain ranges to reservoir views, and the variety of routes suitable for different skill levels.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see near the mountain bike trails?
A: While riding, you might encounter various points of interest. Nearby attractions include the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3895596" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Saltillo Suspension Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3256393" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of La Maroma Massif and Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/388446" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hundred-Spout Fountain&lt;/a&gt;. The charming &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/394693" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Comares Village&lt;/a&gt; is also a significant highlight in the area.

Q: Are there mountain bike routes that offer views of La Viñuela Reservoir?
A: Yes, several routes provide stunning views of La Viñuela Reservoir. A moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31529168" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint – La Viñuela Reservoir loop from El Chiliano&lt;/a&gt;, which offers picturesque vistas of the water and surrounding landscape.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Periana?
A: Periana generally enjoys a Mediterranean climate, making spring and autumn ideal for mountain biking with pleasant temperatures and less extreme weather. Summers can be very hot, so early mornings or late afternoons are recommended if riding during this season. Winters are typically mild, allowing for year-round riding, though some higher mountain paths might be cooler.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that pass through the Zafarraya Plain?
A: Yes, you can explore the Zafarraya Plain on a mountain bike. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21830927" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Polje de Zafarraya – Zafarraya Plain loop from Zafarraya&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 27.0-mile (43.5 km) trail that leads through this distinctive agricultural area.
tips: This bridge and the small, almost unpopulated village to which it gives access, receive their name from the trough that was used to water the cattle that came from transhumance. There is still a large spring used by the few herds that still pass through here.

Old train tracks that came from Vélez-Málaga and that used rack technology in the last section between La Viñuela, Periana and Ventas de Zafarraya. Rack trains had sprockets at the front that hooked into a third rail to navigate steep inclines.

This route is mentioned in the work "The Face of Spain" by Gerald Brenan: "Past Vélez, the train began to climb on a rack railway. All afternoon it meandered between hills of reddish earth covered with vineyards, then descended to with devilish speed, and climbed back towards the pass."
One of the best viewpoints in the Axarquía region, with views of the Viñuela Reservoir and, on clear days, the sea in the background, 25 km away.

faqs.question: What is the technical difficulty of this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a moderate mountain bike ride. It requires good fitness and advanced riding skills, corresponding approximately to a Single Track Scale (STS) S2 technical difficulty. Some less experienced riders might find certain sections challenging enough to require pushing their bike.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, utilizing the historic 'antiguo tren cremallera' (old rack railway). You'll encounter a mix of dirt tracks ('carril terrizo'), royal paths ('realenga'), and sections of the old train route itself. Given its history as a rack railway, expect noticeable ascents and descents throughout the ride.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the old rack railway route?
faqs.answer: The route offers several notable features. You'll cross the impressive El Aguadero Bridge and ride along the historic Old Rack Railway Route (Vía Verde de la Axarquía). There's also a fantastic View of Embalse de la Viñuela from Periana, which is considered one of the best viewpoints in the Axarquía region.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: You can expect breathtaking scenery and stunning views. The trail winds through olive groves, almond trees, and fig trees, offering panoramic vistas of the Viñuela Reservoir. On clear days, you might even spot the Mediterranean Sea in the distance. The area is part of the Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama Natural Park, characterized by deep valleys, mountain ranges, and spectacular wild nature, including charming white villages.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail in Periana?
faqs.answer: As this is a circular route starting and ending in Periana, you can typically find parking within the village itself. Specific parking areas for trail access are usually well-marked or can be found near the main access points to the old rack railway route.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the 'Sierra de Alhama – circular desde Periana por antiguo tren cremallera' is a circular route, meaning it starts and ends in Periana, bringing you back to your starting point without needing to retrace your steps.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to ride this trail is generally during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months. During these periods, the weather is typically milder and more pleasant for mountain biking, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential winter rains.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the trail?
faqs.answer: As the trail passes through the Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama Natural Park, dogs are generally permitted but must be kept on a leash at all times to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Please ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to access the trail or the Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Typically, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to ride this trail or to enter the Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama Natural Park. However, always respect park regulations, such as staying on marked paths and not disturbing the natural environment.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers sections of the [CIMA ML04] Puerto del Sol (27%), Ruta de la Sierra (18%), and the Andalucía - South Route (Provincia de Málaga) (15%). You may also encounter parts of the [CIMA ML14] Boquete de Zafarraya and Perina - Pulgarín trails.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and length, it's essential to bring plenty of water, snacks, a helmet, appropriate mountain biking gear, a repair kit for your bike, and sun protection. A map or GPS device is also recommended, as well as a fully charged phone for emergencies.

waypoints.name: Old Rack Railway Route (Vía Verde de la Axarquía)
View of Embalse de la Viñuela from Periana
